UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the LotoHub application, storing their profile information and assigned role within the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 AuthenticationSystem)

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The assigned role of the user within the system (e.g., 'SuperAdmin', 'AdminBanca', 'Promotor', 'Cambista').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Account.title: Account
Account.description: Represents a specific financial account (e.g., main balance, bonus balance) belonging to a user, storing its current balance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Account entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that owns this account. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Account)

accountType.description: The type of the financial account (e.g., 'Main' for primary balance 'Saldo', 'Bonus' for promotional funds 'Bônus').

balance.description: The current monetary balance available in this account.

currency.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the balance (e.g., 'BRL').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the account was last updated.

Terminal.title: Terminal
Terminal.description: Represents a physical or logical terminal within the LotoHub system, identified by a unique name and associated with an operator.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Terminal entity.

name.description: A descriptive name or identifier for the terminal (e.g., 'Terminal 1234').

location.description: The physical location or contextual description of where the terminal operates.

status.description: The current operational status of the terminal (e.g., 'Online', 'Offline', 'Maintenance').

operatorUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity currently operating or responsible for this terminal. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Terminal)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the terminal record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the terminal record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information, including their full name and current role. The document ID `{userId}` corresponds to the `UserProfile.id` field. Includes a 'role' field for display, while primary authorization checks for roles are handled by dedicated role collections.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores specific financial accounts (e.g., 'Main' for Saldo, 'Bonus' for Bônus) for a user. The `userId` from the parent path is denormalized into the `Account.userId` field for authorization independence. The document ID `{accountId}` corresponds to the `Account.id` field.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this account.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific financial account.

definition.description: A top-level collection for managing all operational terminals. The `operatorUserId` field is denormalized within the document for authorization independence, allowing operators to manage their assigned terminals. The document ID `{terminalId}` corresponds to the `Terminal.id` field.

params.name: terminalId
params.description: The unique ID of the terminal.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for SuperAdmin roles. The *existence* of a document where `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid` grants SuperAdmin privileges. This implements the 'Existence over Content' DBAC model for robust authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user designated as a SuperAdmin.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for AdminBanca roles. The *existence* of a document where `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid` grants AdminBanca privileges. Used for robust authorization checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user designated as an AdminBanca.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for Promoter roles. The *existence* of a document where `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid` grants Promoter privileges. Used for robust authorization checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user designated as a Promoter.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for Cambista roles. The *existence* of a document where `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid` grants Cambista privileges. Used for robust authorization checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user designated as a Cambista.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for LotoHub prioritizes Authorization Independence, DBAC (Database Access Control) using the 'Existence over Content' model for roles, and QAP (Queries as a Predicate) support. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
1.  **User Accounts:** Each `Account` document (e.g., `saldo`, `bônus`) explicitly includes the `userId` (`Account.userId`). This `userId` field is denormalized from its parent `UserProfile` and directly matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path `/userProfiles/{userId}/accounts/{accountId}`. This design eliminates the need for Firestore security rules to use slow and non-atomic `get()` operations on the parent document to verify ownership. Rules can simply check `request.auth.uid == userId` or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` directly against the document and path, ensuring atomic authorization checks during creation, read, update, and delete operations.
2.  **Terminals:** The `Terminal` entity includes an `operatorUserId` field. This field is denormalized within the `Terminal` document itself. When an operator attempts to interact with a terminal, security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.operatorUserId` without needing to fetch any related user or role documents, thus maintaining authorization independence and atomicity.
3.  **Roles (DBAC):** Rather than relying on a `role` field within the `UserProfile` document for *authorization* (which can be vulnerable to spoofing during writes), dedicated top-level collections are created for each role type (e.g., `/superAdmins/{userId}`, `/adminBancas/{userId}`). The *existence* of a document with `request.auth.uid` as its ID in these collections serves as the authoritative source for role-based authorization. This strictly adheres to the 'Existence over Content' principle, making authorization checks highly secure, explicit, and independent of any other document's content, relying solely on `request.auth.uid` and document existence. The `UserProfile.role` field is retained for display and data consistency but not as the primary source for security rules.

**QAPs (Queries as a Predicate) Support:**
1.  **User Profiles (`/userProfiles/{userId}`):** This structure naturally supports QAPs. An individual user can only query (get/update) their own profile (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Administrators (e.g., SuperAdmin, AdminBanca) can be granted permission to list all user profiles, which is a common administrative QAP. 
2.  **User Accounts (`/userProfiles/{userId}/accounts/{accountId}`):** Users can list their own accounts securely as the `{userId}` in the path aligns with `request.auth.uid`. For administrative needs (e.g., AdminBanca viewing all accounts), a Collection Group query on `accounts` is supported, allowing broad queries while still enforcing security rules (e.g., `allow list: if isAdminBanca();`).
3.  **Terminals (`/terminals/{terminalId}`):** Operators can list only the terminals where `operatorUserId == request.auth.uid`, effectively implementing a secure filtered list based on ownership. Administrators can list all terminals. This flexible structure ensures that both specific and broad listing operations adhere to security rules.
4.  **Role Collections:** These collections are primarily accessed via `exists()` checks in security rules, which are inherently QAP-compliant. Administrative users could be allowed to list these collections to manage role assignments.

**Overall Strategy:** The design leverages hierarchical paths for user-owned data (`/userProfiles/{userId}/accounts/{accountId}`), top-level collections for global entities (`/terminals/{terminalId}`), and dedicated single-document collections for roles (`/superAdmins/{userId}`). This promotes structural segregation, clear intent, and robust security rules by minimizing dependencies and explicitly modeling access patterns, adhering to all core design principles.
